content: Hi, I want to use a function which is defined in other rust module, can I use the rust module as a crate? Or I have to declare the function by extern "Rust"? I am not sure it works or not. But I cannot use types defined in that module directly in such way.
Hey, do you mean you have two drivers and want to share some code between them? If so, the best thing to do for now is probably to put that shared code into the kernel crate

Trevor Gross said:

Hey, do you mean you have two drivers and want to share some code between them? If so, the best thing to do for now is probably to put that shared code into the kernel crate

Not only share some code, there are some other cases, like driver a has a queue, and driver b wanna push some into the queue using the interface provided by a.
Iirc you can just put a static in one, and define an extern static in the other
But what exactly is this implementing, why two drivers?

Trevor Gross said:

Iirc you can just put a static in one, and define an extern static in the other

Thanks!

Trevor Gross said:

But what exactly is this implementing, why two drivers?

These drivers are just for different logics instead of real devices. We'd like to define some tracepoints and register the handlers written in rust.I'm just trying to determine if they can be separated, or implement in a single driver if not.
Probably much less confusing to have a single module if at all possible
Especially if they share some specific logic

I'm just trying to determine if they can be separated, or implement in a single driver if not.

It is not supported currently, but the plan is to support it.

Miguel Ojeda said:

I'm just trying to determine if they can be separated, or implement in a single driver if not.

It is not supported currently, but the plan is to support it.

So I cannot use the symbol from other rust modules just by extern item for now?
I forget where I read that all symbols are exported as GPL. Maybe the symbols in the Rust Module are not included ? is there any way to use EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL in the Rust Module?

mu00 said:

I tried to use extern but failed

I got an error like modpost: "TEST" undefined!
Ah, yeah, you would have to work around export.h for now probably
Your best bet is probably to start with a single module at least for development. It can always be split later
